content.claim: Administrative desegregation rules (enrollment/districting, progression guarantees) and centralization of school governance (2013) produced only partial or null effects: segregation in urban areas declined then stagnated by mid-decade, and the 2013 centralization reform did not decrease student achievement inequalities.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Measures were implemented without eliminating track selection, limiting their potential scope
Causality of centralization's null effect on inequality attributed to single study line (Hermann & Semjén)
Time periods of these reforms do not overlap fully; cannot establish interaction effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
